claim: Building AI agent capabilities incrementally (step-by-step) prevents catastrophic failures and allows recovery when problems occur
actionable: Start with minimal permissions and workflows, test thoroughly at each stage, then move to the next stage rather than all-at-once deployment
source_title: I Gave an AI Agent the Keys to My Life (Here's What Happened)

claim: Knowledge bases (like Obsidian with semantic search) enable agents to provide context-aware help and surface relevant past work automatically
actionable: Set up a markdown-based knowledge base with semantic indexing and automatic context linking so agents can reference it when responding to queries
source_title: I Gave an AI Agent the Keys to My Life (Here's What Happened)

claim: Automated overnight operations (cron jobs for backups, index refreshes, updates) can improve system reliability without human intervention during sleep
actionable: Configure automated cron jobs for maintenance tasks like backups, index rebuilds, and version updates with verification scripts before restart
source_title: I Gave an AI Agent the Keys to My Life (Here's What Happened)

claim: Critical safety rules should be placed directly in the agent's main instruction file (soul.md) rather than reference files, as agents are lazy about loading supplementary documents
actionable: Put non-negotiable security rules (like RLS checks) in your main agent.md file where they cannot be skipped
source_title: I Gave an AI Agent the Keys to My Life (Here's What Happened)

claim: AI agents benefit from opinionated guidance about workflows and best practices for a specific domain rather than just comprehensive feature lists
actionable: When creating agent instructions, include recommended workflows and best practices for your domain, not just API documentation
source_title: I Gave an AI Agent the Keys to My Life (Here's What Happened)
